name: DB2 Common Server
lifecycle_note: Continues as IBM Db2; current versions available for cloud and on-premises

name: DB2/MVS
lifecycle_note: Continues as IBM Db2 for z/OS (mainframe)

name: DB2 Parallel Edition
lifecycle_note: Capabilities absorbed into mainstream IBM Db2

name: SQL
lifecycle_note: SQL remains the dominant relational database query language; ANSI/ISO standard continuously updated

name: DRDA (Distributed Relational Database Architecture)
lifecycle_note: DRDA largely superseded by modern REST APIs and JDBC/ODBC connectivity; still used in some IBM mainframe environments

name: ODBC
lifecycle_note: ODBC remains a widely used database connectivity standard

name: X/Open CLI
lifecycle_note: X/Open CLI largely superseded by JDBC and modern ORMs; ODBC descended from it

name: DataPropagator
lifecycle_note: DataPropagator functionality absorbed into IBM InfoSphere Data Replication and IBM Data Replication products

name: DataJoiner
lifecycle_note: DataJoiner functionality absorbed into IBM InfoSphere Federation Server

name: DataHub
lifecycle_note: DataHub replaced by IBM Data Management Console and other tools

name: DataRefresher
lifecycle_note: Superseded by modern ETL tools

name: DB2 Visualizer
lifecycle_note: Superseded by modern BI tools

name: DataBasic (Stored Procedure Builder)
lifecycle_note: Superseded by modern DB2 development tools

name: VisualAge
lifecycle_note: VisualAge superseded by IBM Rational and Eclipse-based tools

name: OS/2 Warp
lifecycle_note: OS/2 discontinued; IBM ended OS/2 support in December 2006

name: AIX
lifecycle_note: AIX remains IBM's enterprise Unix OS for Power Systems

name: Windows NT
lifecycle_note: Windows NT superseded by Windows 2000 and later Windows Server versions

name: IBM RS/6000
lifecycle_note: RS/6000 line evolved into IBM Power Systems

name: IBM RS/6000 SP
lifecycle_note: RS/6000 SP evolved into IBM Blue Gene and later HPC platforms

name: TCP/IP
lifecycle_note: TCP/IP remains the dominant networking protocol

name: SNA LU6.2 (APPC/APPN)
lifecycle_note: SNA largely replaced by TCP/IP in enterprise networks

name: CICS
lifecycle_note: CICS remains active as IBM CICS Transaction Server

name: Encina
lifecycle_note: Encina TP monitor discontinued after IBM acquired Transarc

name: Tuxedo
lifecycle_note: Tuxedo still active; transferred through BEA Systems to Oracle

name: ADSM (Adstar Distributed Storage Manager)
lifecycle_note: ADSM rebranded as IBM Tivoli Storage Manager, later IBM Spectrum Protect

name: REXX
lifecycle_note: REXX remains supported on IBM platforms and continues in use

name: RACF
lifecycle_note: RACF remains IBM's mainframe security manager

name: SNMP
lifecycle_note: SNMP remains widely used for network management

name: PowerBuilder
lifecycle_note: PowerBuilder continues under SAP (via Sybase acquisition); current versions available

name: Lotus Approach
lifecycle_note: Lotus Approach discontinued; Lotus suite absorbed into IBM products

name: Lotus Notes
lifecycle_note: Lotus Notes/Domino continues as HCL Notes/Domino after HCL acquired IBM Collaboration Portfolio in 2019

name: NotesPump
lifecycle_note: NotesPump functionality absorbed into later IBM/HCL replication tools

name: IBM FlowMark
lifecycle_note: FlowMark evolved into IBM MQSeries Workflow and later IBM Business Process Manager

name: HACMP
lifecycle_note: HACMP continues as IBM PowerHA SystemMirror

name: TPC-C
lifecycle_note: TPC-C benchmark still used for OLTP performance measurement
